app.title: Word Hunter
app.tagline: App to track new words to learn
app.footer: Data is stored in this browser.
app.description: Word Hunter — local Rust/Tauri reader, vocabulary trainer, and offline translator.

topbar.newTooltip: Words added in the current session

onboarding.languageCopy: Pick the app language and the language profile you want to learn first. You can change both later in Settings.

import.heading: Import
import.fileHint: UTF-8 text files, subtitle files .srt, .vtt, .ass, .epub, and PDFs with a text layer or desktop OCR are supported. MOBI/AZW works when Calibre (ebook-convert) is installed. Scanned PDF OCR uses the bundled native PaddleOCR runtime.
import.mobileFileHint: On Android, import accepts EPUB, lightweight UTF-8 text files, subtitle files .srt, .vtt, .ass, and PDFs with a text layer. Move scanned PDFs and MOBI/AZW from PC through sync.
import.youtubeChooseTrack: Found {count} subtitle tracks. Choose one and import.
import.youtubeNoTracks: No captions found for this video.
import.title: Title
import.author: Author (optional)
import.textPlaceholder: Paste text or load a file above...
import.pdfPocketScanTitle: Scanned PDFs need the PC version
import.pdfPocketScanBody: Word Hunter Pocket can import PDFs that already contain selectable text. This file appears to be scanned. Import it in Word Hunter on your PC for OCR, then sync it to Pocket.
import.ocrTakingLonger: Taking longer than estimated ({elapsed}) — still working…
import.ocrWholeBookConfirm: OCR will process the whole book. Large PDFs may take many hours — keep the app open.
import.ocrWholeBookStatus: Processing the whole book… {elapsed} elapsed. This may take a long time; keep the app open.
import.pdfTextLayerBlurb: Imported from a PDF text layer ({pages} pages). Scanned PDFs need OCR in the desktop app.

discover.heading: Book search
discover.sortHint: “Author year” is the author's birth year — Gutendex doesn't expose publication year.
discover.levelHint: Heuristic: filters Project Gutenberg by topic
discover.selectionHint: Tick books to add several at once.
discover.emptyPrompt: Enter a phrase and click “Search” to query the Project Gutenberg catalog.
discover.infoBody: Search uses the open gutendex.com API. Once added, metadata is stored locally and the text is fetched from Project Gutenberg on demand.

settings.learningHint: Changing the language switches the active vocabulary and library database.
settings.wordPanelItemsHint: Choose which items appear and use Up or Down to set their order. Hidden actions keep their keyboard shortcuts.
settings.themeHint: The theme is shared by all learning languages. Familiar themes stay dark on desktop and follow the system mode on Pocket.
settings.cardStatsHint: Shows each book's reading progress and vocabulary count in the Library cards.
settings.cardStatsModeHint: Show percentages, exact occurrence counts, or both.
settings.coversHint: Shows book cover thumbnails when Gutenberg metadata provides one; books without covers stay text-only.
settings.statusSoundsHint: Plays a different short sound for New, Learning, Known, and Ignored.
settings.removalBehaviorHint: Ignore keeps the entry and hides it from learning. Delete removes it completely, so the word appears as New again.
settings.autoTtsOnWordFocusHint: Plays pronunciation whenever word focus changes by click or keyboard navigation.
settings.ttsWordHighlightHint: Shows a yellow outline on the word currently being read when the TTS engine reports word timing.
settings.touchControlsHint: On desktop, makes buttons and spacing roomier for touch screens, laptops, and tablets.
settings.readerFocusModeHint: On desktop, hides the top bar, book metadata, and shortcut hints to leave more room for text.
settings.readerWordPanelVisibleHint: Lets you hide the right panel and read at full width when you do not need it.
settings.highlightHint: Underlines saved words in the reader using their learning-status colors. Turn off for plain text.
settings.autoLearnHint: First click on an unknown word immediately saves it as Learning instead of leaving it New.
settings.dataFolderHint: Choose where WordHunter keeps its local data. Keep this folder on the device for the safest startup.
settings.dataFolderSteps: WordHunter can prepare its own sync folder and keep it updated from the local app data.
settings.dataFolderCloudDelay: Cloud copies should mirror that WordHunter folder. Direct cloud mounts can be delayed or incomplete, so keep files available offline before using another device.
settings.dataFolderConfirm:
Choose where WordHunter stores local data.

The safest option is a folder on this device. Choose a sync folder with the separate button.

Continue?
settings.cloudSyncUnavailable: Cloud sync is not available yet. Check the connector status.
settings.androidDataFolderFixed: On Android, local data stays in the protected app folder. Choose a separate sync folder instead.
settings.syncStatusDefault: Data merge status: local app data only.
settings.syncStatusReady: Data merge status: Syncthing folder selected.
settings.syncStatusError: Data merge status: save failed. Check Syncthing folder access.
settings.syncHealthCaution: Sync folder health: available, but this looks like a mounted or cloud-backed folder.
settings.syncHealthNeedsAttention: Sync folder health: {issues} file issues need review.
settings.syncHealthReadOnly: Sync folder health: WordHunter cannot write to this folder.
settings.syncHealthMissing: Sync folder health: configured folder is missing.
settings.syncHealthNotFolder: Sync folder health: configured path is not a folder.
settings.cloudSyncStatusComplete: Cloud sync: last sync complete for {remote}.
settings.cloudSyncStatusNotSupported: Cloud sync: not available on this platform yet.
settings.cloudSyncStatusAuthRequired: Cloud sync: sign in again with Connect Google Drive.
settings.cloudSyncStatusError: Cloud sync: failed. Check the connector status.
settings.syncthingPairPrompt: Enter the device ID from the other device:
settings.syncthingQRHint: Scan this QR code in Syncthing on the other device.
settings.syncWizStep1Desc: WordHunter will create a WordHunterSync folder and copy your data there. This is the shared folder between devices.
settings.syncWizStep2Desc: Syncthing automatically syncs files between devices. WordHunter can start it in the background.
settings.syncWizStep3Desc: Scan the QR code on the other device or enter the device ID manually. Both devices need Syncthing running.
settings.syncWizStep4Desc: WordHunter Pocket does not include Syncthing. Install Syncthing Fork on the phone, then use the QR code or Device ID shown by WordHunter on the PC.
settings.syncWizAndroid1: Install Syncthing Fork from Play Store or F-Droid.
settings.syncWizAndroid2: Open Syncthing Fork and tap + on the Devices tab.
settings.syncWizAndroid3: On the PC, open WordHunter > Sync, start Syncthing, and click Show my QR code. Scan that code or enter the PC Device ID.
settings.syncWizAndroid4: In WordHunter Pocket, prepare the sync folder and choose the same folder as Syncthing.
settings.syncWizFinalActive: Syncthing is running. This device ID starts with {deviceId}; connected peers: {peers}.
settings.syncWizFinalNoPeers: Syncthing is running. This device ID starts with {deviceId}; add another device to start syncing.
settings.syncConflictRefresh: Run sync again to load conflict details.
settings.recoveryPendingSaveTemp: Interrupted save temp file is present.
settings.recoveryQuarantinedJournal: A corrupt interrupted-save journal was quarantined.
settings.syncFolderChangeFailed: Could not set the sync folder.
settings.syncFolderChangeFailedDetail: Could not set the sync folder: {error}
settings.clearWordsHint: Removes all saved words, translations, and learning progress for this language.
settings.clearLibraryHint: Removes all custom texts and resets the book list while keeping your word database.
settings.dynamicLearningColors: Learning word color by SRS level
settings.dynamicLearningColorsHint: Level 1 starts orange and level 5 ends green. Adjust the colors below.
settings.inTextReviewHint: For Learning words, guess the translation first, then rate your recall from 1 to 5. (Reader review becomes available no sooner than the following day after first learning the word.)
settings.hideKnownIgnoredHint: Leaves known and ignored words unmarked, so only words that still need attention stand out.
settings.wordAlgorithmHint: New detects languages without spaces and short subtitle lines better, for example Japanese or Chinese. Classic splits only letter runs, so it can be more predictable for simple texts.
settings.srsAlgorithmHint: SM-2 schedules from your last grade and ease factor. FSRS estimates stability and difficulty from review history, so due dates can change more aggressively.
settings.backupHint: JSON contains settings, language profiles, vocabulary, custom texts, and hidden books. Importing JSON replaces the current app state with data from the file.
settings.ankiHint: TSV exports words, translations, the first context, and optional articles. Importing TSV adds or updates vocabulary, but does not transfer settings, books, or profiles.
settings.resetHint: Resets theme, reader, dictionary, TTS, and other preferences to defaults. It does not delete words or texts.
settings.clearHint: Clears the local app database: vocabulary, custom texts, settings, and progress. Export JSON first if you want a backup.
settings.autoAddLearningOnlyHint: Only Learning words enter the review queue. New words stay out until you mark them Learning.
settings.autoTranslateHint: When a saved word has an empty translation, fills it with the selected translation engine. Manual translations stay untouched.
settings.useEdgeTtsHint: Generates more natural and fluid speech. Requires internet and sends the read text to Microsoft servers.
settings.offlineTranslatorHint: Enables local CTranslate2 translation for words and full sentences. Works offline after downloading language packs (~100-200MB each).
settings.translationLanguageHint: Enter a language code such as nl, pt-BR, or eo. The pair is shared by every translation engine in this profile.
settings.translationLanguageInvalid: Enter a valid ISO language code, for example nl or pt-BR.
settings.argosAsDictHint: Makes the dictionary button (M) open the built-in offline translator for the selected word instead of your dictionary URL.
settings.argosDownloadHint: Downloads local translation packages for the selected languages, including pairs with English and your learning language when available.
settings.argosDownloadWarning: Note: downloading models will take a while. Do not close the app during the process.
settings.ankiExportStatusesHint: Choose which word statuses are included in the TSV. Anki receives word, translation, context, and optional article columns.

graphs.empty: No vocabulary data yet. Start reading to build your word database.
graphs.dayOfWeek: Review Activity by Day of Week

help.heading: Help and shortcuts
help.shortcutScope: View navigation uses Alt plus the shown letter. Shortcuts pause while you type in a field or while a dialog is open; the editor exceptions are listed below. Reader and flashcard actions take priority over view navigation.
help.pocketIntro: Pocket is designed for touch and a small screen. Desktop keyboard shortcuts do not apply here.
help.pocketControlsBody:
Menu: tap the hamburger in the bottom bar to open the application sections.
Words: tap a word to open its translation, status, notes and review controls. The left and right arrows move the yellow selection and read the next word without opening that panel.
Reader bar: the eye toggles status highlights and Play reads the text from the selected word.
PDF: swipe to change pages; pinch to zoom the visual PDF layer.
help.pocketSyncBody: Pocket stores its private data inside the application. Choose a separate shared folder in Synchronization and use a separately installed Syncthing client to exchange records with a computer. Let synchronization finish and avoid editing the same data on two devices at once.
help.pocketLimitsBody: Pocket does not provide desktop keyboard shortcuts, the desktop data-directory selector, embedded Syncthing controls, local Argos or LM Studio translation, or the desktop OCR runtime. Mobile import supports TXT, Markdown, subtitles, EPUB and PDF; MOBI and AZW are desktop-only. PDF text layers work on Android, while scanned documents may require OCR on a computer before synchronization.
help.tip1: Importing: Desktop accepts UTF-8 text, subtitles, EPUB, PDFs, and MOBI/AZW through Calibre. PDFs with a text layer keep the page image under selectable words; scanned PDF OCR runs in the desktop app through PaddleOCR. Word Hunter Pocket on Android imports EPUB, lightweight text, subtitles, and text-layer PDFs.
help.tip2: Profiles: Changing the target language in Settings creates a completely separate vocabulary list and library.
help.tip3: Dictionary: You can set a custom dictionary URL in Settings. Use {{word}} as a placeholder.
help.tip4: Sync and backup: On Android, local data stays in the app folder. Choose a separate sync folder in Settings to share books and words with desktop. Use JSON export as a manual full backup.
help.tip5:
Status counters:
☆ (purple) — words added in the current session.
Gray number — all words stored in your database.
✎ (blue) — words you are currently learning.
✓ (green) — words fully learned (known).
help.tip6: Graphs tab: Alt + G shows vocabulary progress, review forecasts, status distribution, intervals, ease, daily activity, and heatmaps.
help.tip7: Flashcard chart: In Settings you choose whether flashcards show a heatmap, forecast, intervals, ease, or repetitions.
help.tip8: PDF reader: Use the PDF zoom controls, Ctrl/Meta + mouse wheel, or pinch gesture on Android to inspect page images. The imported PDF text layer is stored with the book, while vocabulary, progress, sync, and backups keep the same local-first data model.
help.creditGutenberg: Project Gutenberg / Gutendex — free public domain book library and metadata search API.
help.creditWikipedia: Wikipedia API — free and fast image search for flashcards in the target learning language.
help.creditYouglish: YouGlish — contextual pronunciation video search widget using the YouGlish API.
help.creditSrs: SM-2 & FSRS — SuperMemo-2 and Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler algorithms for flashcard scheduling.
help.creditSqlite: Local record files & JSON — durable per-record storage for books and vocabulary plus versioned JSON snapshots for preferences and recovery. Word Hunter 1.0 does not use SQLite.
help.creditPyside: Tauri 2 + Rust — native app shell. Desktop uses the system WebView/WebView2, while Word Hunter Pocket on Android runs in Android System WebView with the Rust backend compiled as a native library.
help.creditInter: Inter font — designed by Rasmus Andersson (SIL Open Font License 1.1). Used as the primary UI typeface.
help.contactLicense: License: AGPL-3.0-or-later. Closed-source commercial use of derivative code requires a separate written license. Details: https://github.com/Ironship/WordHunter/blob/main/LICENSE
help.whatsNew: Word Hunter 1.0.7: exact-word bookmarks and Reader position restoration; safer synchronization, backups, and imports; improved large Android syncs and conflict resolution; an Apple Silicon DMG; and stronger Linux packages.
help.focusHint1: Press <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>Enter</kbd> to enter or return to text
help.focusHint9: <kbd>F6</kbd> switch focus between text and Word Panel

navKeys.search: <kbd>/</kbd> - Focus search in Library, Discover, or Vocabulary
navKeys.offlineDictionary: <kbd>M</kbd> - Open the built-in dictionary/translator when enabled and no word action is active
navKeys.escape: <kbd>Esc</kbd> - Clear image results, close YouGlish, or leave the current field/selection

readerKeys.select: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>B</kbd> - Focus the book selector in the desktop Reader
readerKeys.font: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>+</kbd> / <kbd>-</kbd> - reader text size; <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Alt</kbd> + <kbd>+</kbd>/<kbd>-</kbd>/<kbd>0</kbd> - desktop interface scale
readerKeys.focus: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Enter</kbd> - Return to the last selected word in the text
readerKeys.page: <kbd>PgUp</kbd> / <kbd>PgDn</kbd> - change page; <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>G</kbd> - focus the page number field
readerKeys.imgSuggest: <kbd>I</kbd> - Search image, <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>1</kbd>/<kbd>2</kbd>/<kbd>3</kbd> - Select suggestion, <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>4</kbd> - Upload own image
readerKeys.inTextReview: <kbd>Enter</kbd> - Show the answer during an in-text review, <kbd>1</kbd>–<kbd>5</kbd> - Rate the answer
readerKeys.activate: <kbd>Enter</kbd> / <kbd>Space</kbd> - Select a focused word; Space reads it when already selected
readerKeys.continuousTts: <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>Space</kbd> - Start or stop continuous TTS from the active word
readerKeys.bookmark: <kbd>B</kbd> - Add or remove a bookmark at the active word

focusKeys.panel: <kbd>F6</kbd> - Switch focus between the text and Word Panel
focusKeys.firstLast: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Home</kbd> / <kbd>End</kbd> - First / last word in the text

actionKeys.merge: <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + Click - Merge selected word with newly clicked word
actionKeys.smartSuggest: <kbd>5</kbd> - Apply "Smart Suggestion" when no in-text rating is visible
actionKeys.status: <kbd>1</kbd> <kbd>2</kbd> <kbd>3</kbd> <kbd>4</kbd> - Change status: New / Learning / Known / Ignored
actionKeys.editFields: <kbd>E</kbd> - Focus translation field, <kbd>N</kbd> - focus note field, <kbd>I</kbd> - search image
actionKeys.removeStatus: <kbd>X</kbd> - Delete the word and all its saved translation, notes, examples, image, and review history

flashcardsKeys.score: <kbd>0</kbd> – <kbd>5</kbd> - Rate word knowledge after showing the answer
flashcardsKeys.searchImage: <kbd>I</kbd> - Search image, <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>1</kbd>/<kbd>2</kbd>/<kbd>3</kbd> - Select a result, <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>4</kbd> - Upload own image

editorKeys.word: Add/edit word: <kbd>1</kbd>–<kbd>4</kbd> selects status while editing a field; <kbd>Enter</kbd> confirms outside the example textarea
editorKeys.book: Edit book: <kbd>Enter</kbd> saves outside a textarea; <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>Enter</kbd> inserts a line; <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>/<kbd>Meta</kbd> + <kbd>V</kbd> can paste an image

help.sm2Body1: Flashcards use FSRS by default, tracking each word's stability and difficulty. You can switch to classic SM-2 in Settings.
help.sm2BodyFsrs: FSRS: ratings update card stability and difficulty, so due dates can adapt more strongly to your review history than SM-2.
help.sm2Grade02: 0-2 (Forgot): The interval resets to 1 day and the word will appear more frequently.
help.sm2Grade3: 3 (Hard): Correct, but the interval grows slowly.
help.sm2Grade5: 5 (Easy): Perfect recall, the interval grows much faster.
help.creditOfflineTranslator: Translation, PDF, and OCR — desktop uses CTranslate2 (MIT), SentencePiece (Apache-2.0), paddle-ocr-rs/PaddleOCR (Apache-2.0), ONNX Runtime (MIT), and PDFium (BSD-3-Clause plus component notices). Windows OCR can use DirectML under Microsoft's redistribution terms.
help.creditSync: Syncthing 2.1.0 — bundled on desktop as a separate executable under MPL-2.0; source: https://github.com/syncthing/syncthing/tree/v2.1.0
help.creditNotices: Complete notices — LICENSE, THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md, and generated Rust dependency license reports are included with every release package.

syncGuide.heading: Step-by-step guide
syncGuide.intro: This tab walks you through syncing a computer and Android phone. Word Hunter does not require an account; devices exchange files through the WordHunterSync folder.
syncGuide.sourceOfTruth: Important: the app's local data is the main source of truth. The sync folder is a copy used to exchange changes between devices. On Android, Word Hunter first copies the selected folder into staging, performs a safe merge, and only then writes the result back.
syncGuide.before1: Export a JSON backup in Settings if you are moving important data for the first time.
syncGuide.before2: Close other apps that could edit the WordHunterSync folder on every device.
syncGuide.before3: Use a folder dedicated to Word Hunter. After reinstalling, you can select the existing folder containing your data again.
syncGuide.before4: If you use cloud storage or Syncthing, wait for the folder to be up to date before opening another device.
syncGuide.before5: Do not choose the app's local data folder as the sync folder. These must be two different places.
syncGuide.pcTitle: PC: prepare the folder and Syncthing
syncGuide.pc1: Click Prepare Syncthing folder. Word Hunter will create or update the WordHunterSync folder.
syncGuide.pc2: Check the status text: the sync folder should be selected and the folder health should be ready.
syncGuide.pc3: Click Start Syncthing. The PC version includes Syncthing inside the app.
syncGuide.pc4: Click Show my QR code and keep the window open if you want to scan this PC from your phone.
syncGuide.pc5: On the phone, open Syncthing-Fork and add a new device by QR code or by entering the PC Device ID.
syncGuide.pc6: When the phone asks for a folder, choose or create WordHunterSync on the phone.
syncGuide.pc7: On the PC, accept the new device if Syncthing asks for confirmation.
syncGuide.pc8: Keep Word Hunter and Syncthing open until Syncthing says the folder is up to date.
syncGuide.pc9: Only then open Word Hunter Pocket on Android and run folder sync there.
syncGuide.android1: Install Syncthing-Fork from Play Store or F-Droid. Word Hunter Pocket itself does not run Syncthing.
syncGuide.android2: In Syncthing-Fork, pair the phone with the PC by QR code or Device ID.
syncGuide.android3: In Syncthing-Fork, set the WordHunterSync folder on the phone and wait until files are downloaded.
syncGuide.android4: Open Word Hunter Pocket and go to this Sync tab.
syncGuide.android5: Tap the sync folder picker and choose exactly the same folder that Syncthing-Fork uses.
syncGuide.android6: Android will show a system folder picker. Grant access to the whole folder, not to a single file.
syncGuide.android7: Word Hunter will check that it can read, write, and delete a small test file in that folder.
syncGuide.android8: After the check succeeds, the app copies the folder into staging, merges data, and writes the result back.
syncGuide.android9: When it finishes, return to Syncthing-Fork and wait until it sends changes to the PC.
syncGuide.android10: On the PC, click Merge data now or wait for automatic background merging.
syncGuide.verify1: The status should show the selected WordHunterSync folder.
syncGuide.verify2: Syncthing on the PC should show a connected device.
syncGuide.verify3: Syncthing-Fork on Android should show the folder as up to date.
syncGuide.verify4: A new word added on the PC should appear on Android after both sides sync.
syncGuide.verify5: A new text added on Android should appear on the PC after both sides sync.
syncGuide.verify6: If a conflict appears, do not delete the folder. Use the conflict panel in this tab.
syncGuide.trouble1: If Android does not see changes, open Syncthing-Fork and check whether the folder is up to date.
syncGuide.trouble2: If the PC does not see the phone, make sure both devices are on the same network or have internet access.
syncGuide.trouble3: If the folder is read-only, choose another folder or fix permissions in the Android system picker.
syncGuide.trouble4: If you see old data, wait for Syncthing first, then click Merge data now in Word Hunter.
syncGuide.trouble5: Do not repair sync by manually copying files from the app data folder. Use WordHunterSync.
syncGuide.trouble6: If you have two phones, add them one at a time and check data after each device.
syncGuide.trouble7: Before large imports, many books, or testing a new phone, export a JSON backup.
